source_title: Towards Robotic Dexterous Hand Intelligence: A Survey

ai_summary.one_liner: A holistic survey that connects dexterous hand hardware, sensing, control/learning methods, datasets, and evaluation into one developmental map.
ai_summary.problem: Dexterous hand studies differ in embodiment, sensors, task assumptions, data sources, and evaluation protocols, making progress hard to compare systematically.
ai_summary.solution: The survey analyzes actuation, transmission, perception, representative hands, task categories, policy-learning paradigms, datasets, modalities, and evaluation practice under one taxonomy.
ai_summary.key_result: Its main output is a structured map of the field: hardware choices constrain policy assumptions, learning methods depend on data/evaluation design, and robust deployment requires closing gaps in sensing, sim-to-real transfer, benchmarks, and safety.
limitations: The survey is taxonomy-heavy and does not provide a unified benchmark or meta-analysis across all cited methods.
Fast-moving 2026 dexterous-hand and humanoid-hand releases may outpace the static survey snapshot.

research_gaps.question: Hardware, tactile sensing, and policy learning are often optimized separately.
General dexterous VLA systems still need reliable real-time force and safety grounding.

future_directions: Co-design of hand hardware, tactile sensing, and policy learning.
Standardized dexterous manipulation datasets and evaluation protocols.
Robust sim-to-real pipelines for high-DoF contact-rich hands.
